reviews.review_text:
Directly adjacent to Club, join for $5 to access, in a quiet area of town, with power and water available. $25 night with max 4 nights twice per month.
Amenities are two (2) ensuite style units accessible using fob provided
Most sites level bitumen only using chock to balance our motorhome
DUMP POINT ONSITE
MUST BE FULLY SEMF CONTAINED TO STAY

Not all sites are level. Must become a member of the bowls club across the street to stay ($5/year and worth it). A little tight in spots for big vans but manageable. Just walk to the bowls club for great food, great service and amenities. Park amenities are good, use the shower on the far side.

MUST BE A MEMBER. Motor homes only
MUST BE SELF CONTAINED WITH YOUR OWN. YES YOUR OWN SHOWER AND TOILET.
NOTHING SUPPLIED. BYO. $15 A NIGHT FOR A PARKING SPOT.
NO camper trailers.
NO tents.
Only motor homes.
Walk to shops good stop over
Another year and another stay at Wanderest. An excellent place to stop. Very easy, quiet and relaxing.  Thanks folks.

We stayed for 1 night after having to become a member at club, that’s ok.  My review is more about the amenities block.  When was it cleaned last?   6 months, 1 year.   Disgraceful,  black mould all over shower ceiling and filthy showers and floors.   We wouldn’t stay there again !
Great place to stay at a very reasonable price.

Nice Weather, good view, enjoy your trip @ relax 😎 time to travel 🧳..

Thanks 🙏🏻

Good spot to stop for a night or two. Facilities are old but adequate. Convenient location to shopping and laundry. Brilliant club (The Richmond Club) straight across the road which has two top notch restaurants.  One Italian and one Asian. Worth the stay just for these.
Good short stay! Close to everything! Club is great!
Door on shower block opened and beautiful shower inside

Safe, off-road parking, in a quite area of town, with power and water available. Amenities are a bit limited - just two (2) ensuite style units available, which may struggle if the park is busy. Reasonable rates and very close the the Richmond Club for a drink or a feed.

Very basic facilities but good enough for nights stay, no level sites but except for one area, cheap at 25 bucks powered site
Run by rsl opposite with good bar and Resto
Great stop over with or without power will stay again

Wanderest is well-priced for what it is, and surprisingly attractive. Although it's basically just a tarred carpark with power and shared water, it's tree-lined and overlooks playing fields and paddocks, so the outlook is better than you might expect. The dump point and a couple of showers and toilets are useful extras, and it's walking distance to club and shops. Kids will enjoy the occasional RAAF planes flying low overhead 🙂

A long power lead is advised; on some sites it can be well over 10m between you and the power pole.
Are the signs anything to go by with the state of the park?? Little maintenance it seems!

This was an unused carpark from the bowls club across the road I think. There were some sites with power and water. There are also showers, toilets and a dump point available. We had an unpowered site. It was on bitumen. It was a very pleasant  stay and cost $10/night but you had to be a member of the bowls club. It was only an extra $5. We visited the club each day after we'd been out. Happy hour was on so drinks were a good price. Staff were friendly and professional.
Comfortable short stay camping. Amazingly low prices for a Sydney camping spot.
It's not a flash setup and the bottom sites (unpowered) are quite steep so levelling takes some work. Average facilities as well. However for the price, this place is fantastic.
Excellent value. Great location for Sydney. Club is friendly and does good meals. Great hot showers. Dump point.
A caravan park.
